examples: That means that they live life "energetically"—more intensively, but not for as long.
Points on the skin are energetically connected to specific organs, body structures and systems.
Those devout traders, called "Wali Ullah" or "those close to God", energetically spread both trade and religion by adapting their appeals tothe native mysticism of Java.
It was energetically publicised (albeit in caustic terms) by two Salafist (hardline Islamist) television channels.
The difference is that, thanks to Sarbanes-Oxley, regulators are now enforcing the rules more energetically.
